_note: PROPOSAL-ONLY sidecar for the whole-estate insights review (docs/agent-whole-estate-insights-review.md). Shaped like censor/insights-suggestions.jsonl entries but kept OUT of that ledger because that ledger's rows are keyed by Claude-snapshot `reports` stamps and are read by insight-cadence.py's coverage-gap check; cross-vendor rows would corrupt that join. Nothing here is armed or consumed by any organ.

proposals.finding: Session-abandonment is systemic across the estate but wears a different face per vendor: Claude stalls mid-session (24 stall-sessions; persisting closeout loop), Codex abandons whole sessions after one entry (16.6%), OpenCode rapid-abandons half its sessions inside 30s (55%). The disciplines that fix this (closeout terminality, done-as-predicate, registry-owns-answer, scoped verification) exist only for the Claude surface.
evidence: PR #1122 (Session Discipline shared layer + check M — discipline parity across all 10 agent surfaces)
proposals.review: empirical close = PR #1122 merged and check M green across the agent-doc surfaces; cross-vendor abandonment rates fall in the next ingest window

proposals.finding: Require an owner-scope + predicate + expected-receipt handshake before an OpenCode/Codex session mutates tasks.yaml or code, mirroring docs/agent-opencode-review.md Improvements #1 and the ask-gate discipline. Refuses to start unbounded work, targeting the 16.6% Codex single-entry and 55% OpenCode rapid-abandon rates.
proposals.review: proposal-only; arming is a future lever, not an auto-enable

proposals.finding: Promote scripts/insight-cross-vendor-ingest.py (PR #1124) to a registered beat sensor in institutio/governance/sensors.yaml so per-vendor friction counts refresh every cadence and feed the censor cascade the way insights-drift.json already does for the Claude surface.
proposals.review: proposal-only; arming is a lever, not an auto-enable

data_gaps.gap: Adapter pattern-matches the SQLite JSON blob and cannot distinguish an empty/errored zero-cost session from real work lost to a flaky free provider. The 55% rapid-abandon / 100% zero-cost numbers are therefore unreadable as either cheap-ignore or expensive-alarm.
data_gaps.owner: future revision to scripts/insight-cross-vendor-ingest.py (bounded PII-safe blob parse: classify each zero-cost session empty / errored / completed-free)

data_gaps.gap: Adapter counts 1,793 tool errors across a capped 500-session sample (>=3.6/session, a floor) but does not classify them (permission-denial vs file-race vs network/MCP vs speculative-cheap), so a real regression is inseparable from expected speculative noise.
data_gaps.owner: future follow-up sensor to bucket Claude tool-error classes; would also make the root-to-leaf-permission discipline measurable
